column7: If the owner is essential to daily operations, the business is not transferable. This is the single strongest buyer-risk indicator.
Materially sub-par profitability with no clear improvement path prevents premium exit outcomes regardless of other strengths.
Active disputes or significant regulatory exposure commonly reduce price, create escrow holdbacks, or kill deals outright.
If the business is more than 3 years from transaction readiness, the current quality of the enterprise is not yet sale-ready.
High concentration is one of the most common valuation discounts because revenue is not viewed as durable.
A business cannot scale sustainably if it depends entirely on the owner's personal capacity.
If the operating model cannot scale without disproportionate cost, growth destroys value rather than creating it.
This should become the top recommendation regardless of score elsewhere — it is the core issue for this goal.
